description:
You are working as a localization engineer on a small open-source project located under
/home/user/project.

1. Translation catalog  
   • Path: /home/user/project/i18n/en.yaml  
   • Current contents (2-space indentation):
     ```
     # English (United States) translations
     welcome: "Welcome"
     login: "Log in"
     profile: "Profile"
     settings: "Settings"
     help: "Help"
     ```
   The product owner has added a “Log out” user-interface string that must now be exposed to translators.

2. Build configuration  
   • Path: /home/user/project/config.toml  
   • Current contents:
     ```
     # Project build configuration
     [translations]
     supported = 5           # number of keys in the en.yaml catalogue
     
     [meta]
     last_updated = "2023-09-01"
     ```
   Once the new string is added, the supported counter has to be increased to 6 and the last_updated
   timestamp must be set to "2023-10-31".

Goal  
Update BOTH files so that:
• /home/user/project/i18n/en.yaml gains one new root-level key named logout whose value
  is the exact string "Log out".  
• /home/user/project/config.toml changes exactly as follows:
  – In the [translations] section, supported is 6 (no surrounding quotes).  
  – In the [meta] section, last_updated is "2023-10-31" (with the double quotes).  

Verification log  
After updating the two files, create a plain-text confirmation log at  
/home/user/project/update_log.txt with the following *single* line of text (no leading or trailing
spaces, no extra lines):
```
en.yaml updated with logout ; config.toml supported=6 last_updated=2023-10-31
```

The automated test will check:
• The exact presence and spelling (including spacing) of the new logout key in en.yaml.  
• That the supported and last_updated values in config.toml are **exactly** as specified above,
  and that no other part of the file changed.  
• That update_log.txt exists, is readable, and contains precisely the one-line message shown.

All modifications must be performed from /home/user in a standard Linux terminal.
A single well-composed shell command is enough to pass, but you may use as many commands
as you like. Work only with existing tools that are typically present in a minimal
Debian-based distribution (e.g., sed, awk, echo, printf, cat).
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:
1. Directory structure:
   /home/user/project/
   ├── i18n/
   │   └── en.yaml      (permissions 0644)
   └── config.toml      (permissions 0644)

2. /home/user/project/i18n/en.yaml exact initial contents:
   # English (United States) translations
   welcome: "Welcome"
   login: "Log in"
   profile: "Profile"
   settings: "Settings"
   help: "Help"

3. /home/user/project/config.toml exact initial contents:
   # Project build configuration
   [translations]
   supported = 5           # number of keys in the en.yaml catalogue

   [meta]
   last_updated = "2023-09-01"

Desired final state after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/project/i18n/en.yaml exact contents:
   # English (United States) translations
   welcome: "Welcome"
   login: "Log in"
   profile: "Profile"
   settings: "Settings"
   help: "Help"
   logout: "Log out"

   (An extra newline at the end of file is acceptable.)

2. /home/user/project/config.toml exact contents:
   # Project build configuration
   [translations]
   supported = 6           # number of keys in the en.yaml catalogue

   [meta]
   last_updated = "2023-10-31"

3. /home/user/project/update_log.txt exact contents (single trailing newline acceptable):
   en.yaml updated with logout ; config.toml supported=6 last_updated=2023-10-31

Permissions for the newly created update_log.txt: 0644 owned by user.

The test harness will check sha256 sums of final files to ensure correctness.
